title: elaeocarpus stipularis var. castaneus
intro: Elaeocarpus stipularis var. castaneus, also known as the Tawau oak or Copperwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae, native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as wounds and ulcers. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, and are often used to promote overall well-being and to support the immune system.
